## What does this PR change?

<!-- One or two sentences. -->

## Pillar

- [ ] Terms — canonical (`terms/`)
- [ ] Terms — BBP (`bbp/`)
- [ ] Terms — regional (`regional/`)
- [ ] Practices (`practices/`)
- [ ] Maturity (`maturity/`)
- [ ] Meta (README / metadata / CONTRIBUTING / templates)

## Type

- [ ] Typo / formatting only
- [ ] New content (regional variant, translation, practice, …)
- [ ] Substantive term-wording change — **link the RFC Discussion:** <!-- #123 -->

## Provenance & safety checklist

- [ ] File moves used `git mv` (blame preserved) — no delete-and-recreate.
- [ ] Any new term file has a `<!-- Provenance — … -->` header.
- [ ] License is CC0, or a non-CC0/externally-authored doc is labelled in its header with attribution.
- [ ] Legal substance of existing terms is unchanged (unless this is the RFC'd purpose of the PR).
- [ ] **If a path was renamed/moved:** downstream impact noted for `disclose/website` (preprocess paths + submodule pin) and/or `policymaker`.
